creator: robak@freebsd.org
text:
> In looking back I see ONE email from June 19th, which I did indeed miss.
> However, there were no entries here in bugzilla which is where I would have
> expected to see them and where I have been looking, waiting patiently while
> at least a couple of versions of this port were released. I did see that you
> officially took the PR on July 1st, and then no entries until today. Don't
> get me wrong. I do appreciate that you did it, I just would have liked to
> have had the opportunity to be part of the public discussion on the changes
> that you proposed.

Well, sorry about that, but like I said, I've sent the emails and there was no reply, so I assumed a not very uncommon situation, where original poster is no longer interested in his submission and I acted in what I understood as best interest of the community, to update, fix and commit the port, even with me as a maintainer. Once again, I can only apologize for that, and make you the maintainer if you're still willing.

> One change that I am most curious about is why you removed the SPDY module
> and changed the OpenSSL requirements related to it.

The SSL options have been grouped for better readibility of the port structure but there should be no other changes in functionality, are you saying you're seeing some error/mistake in the port?
